role: Speaker of Parliament (1992–2000); Head of Supreme Leader's Inspection Office
bio: Ali Akbar Nategh-Nouri served as Speaker of Parliament for two full terms, 1992 to 2000. He was the establishment-backed frontrunner in the 1997 presidential election before being unexpectedly defeated by reformist [Mohammad Khatami](/people/mohammad-khatami) in a landslide. He subsequently served as head of the Inspection [Office of the Supreme Leader](/orgs/office-of-the-supreme-leader)'s office. He represents the traditional conservative wing of Iranian politics.
editorial_notes: Lost 1997 presidential election to Khatami. Head of Supreme Leader Inspection Office from 1999.
